channel.title: Just In
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026, Australian Broadcasting Corporation. All Rights Reserved.

item.title: Fashion designers are experimenting with AI, but not everyone is a fan
item.description: As Australian Fashion Week gets underway in Sydney, local and international designers debate the merits and detractions of using AI in the industry.
item.dc:creator: Jane Rocca

media:group.media:description: Melbourne-based designer Karla Špetić, whose works are seen here at this year's Melbourne Fashion Festival, had mixed results using AI to create clothing.

item.title: 'We were weird': Tim Finn on Split Enz's rise
item.description: Tim Finn on the past, the present and the possible future of Split Enz.
item.dc:creator: Dan Condon

media:group.media:description: Split Enz's latest line-up features Neil and Tim Finn, Eddie Rayner, Noel Crombie, James Milne and Matt Eccles.

item.title: Quiz: How much did Michael Jordan's signed Air Nike 1s sell for in 2020?
item.description: From the Tudor period right through to the 21st century, test your knowledge of the past with this week's history quiz.

media:group.media:description: A pair of basketball legend Michael Jordan's famous Air Jordans from his rookie season.

item.title: Jay Vine escapes serious injury after horrifying crash at Giro d'Italia
item.description: Australian cyclist Jay Vine has sustained a fractured elbow and concussion after suffering his third crash in 13 race days this year.
item.dc:creator: Simon Smale

media:group.media:description: Jay Vine is out of the Giro d'Italia.

item.title: Phoenix makes A-League history with semifinal triumph over Brisbane
item.description: Wellington Phoenix has become the first side from New Zealand to make an A-League grand final after inching past Brisbane Roar in their semifinal.

media:group.media:description: Woods's double fired Wellington into the grand final.

item.title: Netball body suspends woman charged over alleged antisemitic slur at game
item.description: Jewish community leaders say a woman's alleged slur at an under-12 girls netball game in Sydney reflects a troubling pattern playing out on sporting fields.
item.dc:creator: Victoria Pengilley

media:group.media:description: A woman is confronted after allegedly making antisemitic comments during an under-12 girls netball game in Sydney's east.

item.title: Fuel injection as SA announces $40m diesel stockpile
item.description: A strategic diesel reserve of between 10 million and 20 million litres will be established in SA to bolster the supply and provide assurance to farmers, the state government says.
item.dc:creator: Daniel Keane

media:group.media:description: A strategic diesel reserve has been announced for SA in order to boost supplies amid ongoing uncertainty among farmers.

item.title: When Nicole started checking her breasts, she never expected to find a lump
item.description: Breast cancer survivor Nicole Speirs is among thousands of people who turned out for Mother's Day Classic charity fun run events across the country.
item.dc:creator: Penny Travers and Ruby Thomas

media:group.media:description: Nicole Speirs was diagnosed with breast cancer 18 months ago.

item.title: Sabalenka exits Rome with injury concern ahead of French Open
item.description: Women's tennis world number one Aryna Sabalenka has a fortnight to recover from injury before the French Open after her loss in Rome.

media:group.media:description: Aryna Sabalenka says she has a lower back and hip problem ahead of the French Open, after her third round loss in Rome.

item.title: Premier backs away from revealing reasons for backbencher's legal costs
item.description: Tasmania's premier is under fire for backtracking on an undertaking to explain why taxpayers paid a $15,000 legal bill for a backbencher.
item.dc:creator: Sophie Jaggers

media:group.media:description: Tasmanian Premier Jeremy Rockliff with Liberal Member for Lyons Mark Shelton in 2025.

item.title: Man, 18, dies after falling from sixth-floor balcony while fleeing police
item.description: Police say the young man was attempting to escape over the balcony when he fell and sustained critical injuries.

media:group.media:description: Police say the man attempted to flee over a sixth-floor balcony before falling.

item.title: Sunwater chair labels costly dam rebuild 'grossly disproportionate'
item.description: In a letter addressed to the water minister, Sunwater chair Jeff Seeney said the decision to rebuild the dam lacked "any rigour" or "professional credibility".
item.dc:creator: Stephen Clarke and Emma Johnson

media:group.media:description: Sunwater chair Jeff Seeney has written to the Queensland government recommending against rebuilding the troubled Paradise Dam.

item.title: Man charged with gross indecency, rape after 'appalling behaviour' in hotel
item.description: NT Police have charged a 41-year-old man with rape after his "appalling behaviour" inside a hotel room, which authorities allege was one of two domestic violence incidents to occur in Darwin's CBD on the same day.
item.dc:creator: Gemma Ferguson

media:group.media:description: Police in Darwin responded to the two domestic violence incidents, which were unrelated but occurred in the city centre on the same day.

item.title: Person dies after being hit by plane in the US
item.description: A person who jumped a fence at a US airport has been killed after being struck by a Frontier Airlines plane during take-off.

media:group.media:description: A Frontier Airlines plane was evacuated after a person was struck and killed at Denver International Airport.

item.title: Storm snap record losing run as Tigers coach laments 'negative smell'
item.description: The Melbourne Storm are winners for the first time since round two, snapping a club-record losing streak with a big win over the Wests Tigers.
item.dc:creator: Michael Doyle

media:group.media:description: Sualauvi Faalogo suffered a broken cheekbone after clashing heads with a teammate.

item.title: Australians will be among last to disembark hantavirus-hit cruise ship
item.description: Evacuating the cruise ship will be a long and complex operation that will stretch into the next day, according to Spain's health minister.
item.dc:creator: Fergus Gregg and Andrew Thorpe

media:group.media:description: The first group of passengers from the MV Hondius, consisting of Spanish nationals, are transferred to shore.

item.title: Crows surge past Tigers with strong second half, Dees down Eagles
item.description: Adelaide breaks the shackles after the main break to down a plucky Richmond, as the Demons have a comfortable win over the Eagles who lose a key defender to another concussion.
item.dc:creator: Luke Pentony

media:group.media:description: Riley Thilthorpe was one of the leaders as the Crows kicked five unanswered goals in the third quarter against Richmond.

item.title: Missing scientists may represent a 'grave threat to US national security'
item.description: The scientists supposedly all had access to sensitive government research, and the nature of their work has led to speculation that their cases might all be connected.
item.dc:creator: Lewis Wiseman

media:group.media:description: Some of the scientists worked for NASA.

item.title: Climbers winched to safety after overnight ordeal in the Warrumbungles
item.description: A falling rock sparks a tense rescue in the Warrumbungle National Park in central-western New South Wales.
item.dc:creator: Jen McCutcheon

media:group.media:description: The climbers were rescued from a bluff in Warrumbungle National Park.

item.title: Why this ex-Liberal thinks she can unite One Nation and Greens voters
item.description: Charlotte Mortlock believes it is time for Australia to leave the left-right ideology behind and focus on what brings people together instead.
item.dc:creator: David Marchese

media:group.media:description: Former journalist and Liberal staffer Charlotte Mortlock is starting her own political movement

item.title: Family channels grief into thousands of dollars for Leukaemia Foundation
item.description: Elli Wust was pregnant with her first child when she found out her mum had a rare blood cancer. Now she has two children who never got to meet their grandmother, who died at age 58.
item.dc:creator: Vanessa Jarrett

media:group.media:description: Grandaughter Summer Collins and sisters Elli Wust and Jodie Collins honour Christine Gray in an annual Mother's Day event every year, raising money for the Leukaemia Foundation.

item.title: Love story: Love when you don't know how long you've got
item.description: My 14-year-old daughter Pip has Williams syndrome. The future is uncertain and full of heartache, but my love for my baby is not.

media:group.media:description: Kerrie Franc is mum and carer to daughter Pippa, who has Williams syndrome.

item.title: Police questioning fail-to-stop driver after 92yo killed in crash
item.description: A car rolled and smashed into a power pole, killing its elderly passenger, after swerving to avoid a car driving the wrong way on the New England Highway in southern Queensland.
item.dc:creator: Josh Dye

media:group.media:description: Another life has been lost on Queensland's roads after an elderly man was killed near the NSW border.

item.title: Yaraka publican shares why she's selling the town's only pub
item.description: A year after her husband died in a car crash, Gerry Gimblett says it is time to sell the pub they ran for more than a decade.
item.dc:creator: Crispian Yeomans

media:group.media:description: Gerry Gimblett says it has been a privilege to serve behind the bar at the Yaraka Hotel.

item.title: Fans left 'uncomfortable' as referee criticised in bloody heavyweight brawl
item.description: Daniel Dubois stops Fabio Wardley in the 11th round of a brutal contest to take the WBO heavyweight title as observers ringside criticise an "uncomfortable watch".

media:group.media:description: Daniel Dubois is now a two-time world heavyweight champion.
